title: Fix Navbar Visibility – Always Show Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel, and Login/Logout

requirements.summary: Rewrite Navbar.tsx with flex-nowrap layout to ensure all navigation elements are always rendered and never hidden due to overflow or layout issues, with conditional visibility for Dashboard and Admin Panel links based on authentication and admin status
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, 'Home' link is always visible in the navbar.
On desktop, 'Dashboard' link is visible when the user is authenticated and hidden when not authenticated.
On desktop, 'Admin Panel' link is visible when the user is authenticated AND isCallerAdmin returns true; it is hidden for non-admin users.
On desktop, the Login/Logout button is always visible and never clipped or hidden by overflow.
No navbar items are ever hidden due to CSS overflow, flex-wrap, or z-index issues on any screen size.
On mobile, the hamburger icon is always visible and tapping it reliably toggles the mobile menu open/closed.
The mobile menu shows the same conditional link set (Home always, Dashboard if authenticated, Admin Panel if authenticated + isAdmin) plus a Login/Logout button.
The mobile menu has no z-index or overflow conflicts that would cause it to be hidden behind other elements.
The navbar layout does not break or clip items on viewport widths between 320px and 1440px.

file_operations.description: Rewrite the navbar component to use flex-nowrap and overflow-visible on the desktop nav container. Ensure Home link is always rendered. Conditionally render Dashboard link only when identity is non-null (authenticated). Conditionally render Admin Panel link only when identity is non-null AND isCallerAdmin returns true. Ensure the Login/Logout button is always visible on the right side and never wrapped or hidden. On mobile, ensure the hamburger menu includes the same conditional link set (Home always, Dashboard if authenticated, Admin Panel if authenticated + isAdmin) plus the Login/Logout action. Verify the mobile menu has proper z-index (e.g., z-50 or higher) and no overflow conflicts that would hide it behind other elements. Use the useInternetIdentity hook to check authentication status via identity and the useIsCallerAdmin query to check admin status. Ensure all visible links and the Login/Logout button appear in a single horizontal row on desktop (md and above) without overflow or wrapping.

requirements.summary: Fix useIsCallerAdmin query in useQueries.ts to use fresh authenticated actor, principal-based query key, and zero staleTime/gcTime so admin status refreshes immediately after login
acceptanceCriteria: useIsCallerAdmin is not enabled when identity is null/undefined.
useIsCallerAdmin uses an authenticated actor (created with the current identity) to call isCallerAdmin().
The query key includes the principal string so it re-fetches on identity change.
staleTime and gcTime are both set to 0.
After login, the admin check re-fetches automatically without requiring a page refresh.
Non-admin users receive false from the query.

file_operations.description: Modify the useIsCallerAdmin query function to ensure: (1) the query is enabled only when actor is non-null and identity is non-null (add enabled: !!actor && !!identity to the query options), (2) the query key is ['isCallerAdmin', principalString] where principalString is identity.getPrincipal().toString() so it re-fetches whenever the logged-in principal changes, (3) staleTime: 0 and gcTime: 0 are set in the query options so it never returns stale cached results, and (4) the query uses the actor (which is already created with the current authenticated identity in useActor.ts) to call actor.isCallerAdmin(). This ensures the Admin Panel link appears reliably after login without a page refresh.

requirements.summary: Add or verify useEffect in useActor.ts that invalidates all queries when identity changes from null to non-null (user logs in) so navbar state refreshes immediately
acceptanceCriteria: After Internet Identity login completes, all React Query queries (including isCallerAdmin) are invalidated and re-fetched automatically.
The Admin Panel link appears in the navbar for the admin user immediately after login without a page refresh.
The Dashboard link appears for authenticated non-admin users immediately after login without a page refresh.
The Login button changes to a Logout button immediately after login.

file_operations.description: Add or verify a useEffect that watches the identity object and, whenever the identity changes from null/undefined to a non-null value (user has just logged in), calls queryClient.invalidateQueries() to invalidate all queries (including isCallerAdmin) so they immediately re-fetch with the new authenticated identity. This prevents the navbar from showing stale unauthenticated state after login. Ensure the useEffect dependency array includes identity or identity?.getPrincipal().toString() so it triggers on identity change. The queryClient should already be available from @tanstack/react-query.
